content: We can refactor 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems to adopt a dynamic, context-aware tiered indexing architecture inspired by how MemoryBank organizes stored memory to mitigate the long-standing working memory capacity bottleneck for long-horizon agent reasoning. Curiosity-driven exploration signals (a core focus of modern active learning agents) can be repurposed to automatically prioritize and demote memory entries between fast working memory and large-scale persistent storage, eliminating the need for hand-coded memory eviction policies.
